scenario.description: I want to create a parody music video that uses the melody of a popular pop song but changes all the lyrics to make fun of social media culture.
scenario.purpose: Social commentary through parody of the original song
scenario.additional_context: The parody transforms the meaning completely, using the recognizable melody to critique social media addiction.

notes: Campbell v. Acuff-Rose Music (1994) — parody is paradigmatic fair use

scenario.description: I run an online store and want to use a professional photographer's images of my products that she took during a paid photoshoot. The contract didn't specify web usage rights.
scenario.additional_context: The photos were taken by a professional photographer under a contract that covered print usage only.

notes: Non-transformative commercial use of entire creative work with available licensing

scenario.description: I'm a teacher creating a course packet that includes three chapters from a copyrighted textbook for my nonprofit community college class.
scenario.purpose: Educational use in a nonprofit community college course
scenario.additional_context: The textbook publisher offers a course licensing program but it costs $500 per semester.

notes: Michigan Document Services / Cambridge Univ. Press v. Patton line of cases

scenario.description: I want to build a search engine that indexes and displays thumbnail versions of images found across the web, similar to Google Images.
scenario.purpose: Providing an image search index to help users find images across the internet
scenario.additional_context: Images are copied in full but displayed only as small thumbnails with links back to original sources.

notes: Perfect 10 v. Amazon / Kelly v. Arriba Soft

scenario.description: I'm writing a biography of a living musician and want to quote extensively from their unpublished personal letters and diary entries.
scenario.purpose: Biographical research and documentation of the subject's life
scenario.additional_context: The letters are unpublished and the musician has not authorized the biography.

notes: Harper & Row v. Nation Enterprises / Salinger v. Random House

scenario.description: I'm developing a news aggregation app that displays the first paragraph and headline of articles from major news sites.
scenario.purpose: News aggregation and distribution to inform the public
scenario.additional_context: The app displays headlines, first paragraphs, and links back to the original articles.

notes: AP v. Meltwater — news aggregation with available licensing

scenario.description: I am a visual artist creating a series of silkscreen prints based on a photographer's portrait, adding bright colors and graphic elements in the style of pop art, and selling them through galleries.
scenario.purpose: Creating original artwork that comments on celebrity culture through pop art aesthetic
scenario.additional_context: The original photographer licenses the portrait for magazine covers and commercial use. My prints are sold in the same market as licensed prints.

notes: Andy Warhol Foundation v. Goldsmith (2023) — post-Warhol, same-purpose commercial use

scenario.description: I'm training a large language model on copyrighted books, ingesting the full text to learn patterns but never outputting verbatim passages to users.
scenario.purpose: Training AI models to understand language patterns for a commercial chatbot product
scenario.additional_context: The model learns from the text but doesn't store or reproduce the original works. The output is generated text, not copied passages.

notes: Authors Guild v. Google (analogy), NYT v. OpenAI (pending) — highly contested area

scenario.description: I recorded a college lecture where the professor displayed several copyrighted diagrams from a textbook. I want to upload the recording to a free educational YouTube channel.
scenario.purpose: Free educational content sharing for students who cannot attend lectures
scenario.additional_context: The diagrams are incidental to the lecture content, shown briefly on screen. The channel has no ads or monetization.

notes: Nonprofit educational with incidental use of small portion

scenario.description: I want to use a 10-second clip of a copyrighted song as background music in a TikTok video advertising my clothing brand.
scenario.purpose: Background music for social media product advertisement
scenario.additional_context: The song is popular and the clip includes the recognizable chorus hook. TikTok has licensing agreements with some labels but not this one.

notes: Non-transformative commercial use of heart of creative work with available licensing

scenario.description: I run a nonprofit digital archive and want to preserve out-of-print books by scanning them and making them available online to researchers.
scenario.purpose: Preservation and scholarly access to culturally significant out-of-print works
scenario.additional_context: The books are out of print with no available commercial editions. Access is limited to registered researchers.

notes: HathiTrust — archival of full works for transformative purpose

scenario.description: I'm reimplementing the API of a popular programming language's standard library in a competing platform to let developers use their existing skills.
scenario.purpose: Allowing developers to use familiar API declarations in a new computing platform
scenario.additional_context: Only the API declarations (method signatures, class names) are copied, not the implementing code. The new platform is a competitor.
